data.title: GoPro 10-K: Revenue $651.5M, EPS (loss) $(0.59) — FY2025 results
data.description:
GoPro reported FY2025 revenue of $651.5 million and a basic and diluted net loss per share of $(0.59), reflecting softer consumer demand and fewer camera units shipped. The company narrowed its net loss to $(93.5) million from $(432.3) million in FY2024, while operating losses persisted amid restructuring and a goodwill impairment. Revenue declines and margin pressure prompted cost reduction and R&D prioritization ahead of a product rollout planned for 2Q26.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $651.5 million, down 19% year-over-year (FY2025 vs FY2024).
  • Revenue by category: Hardware $545.3 million; Subscription and services $106.3 million.
  • Gross profit: $219.2 million; gross margin 33.6% (FY2025), down 20 basis points versus FY2024.
  • Operating loss: $(83.3) million; operating expenses totaled $302.5 million (46% of revenue), including an $18.6 million goodwill impairment.
  • Net loss: $(93.5) million (improved versus $(432.3) million in FY2024, which included a $294.9 million deferred tax valuation allowance).
  • Basic and diluted net loss per share: $(0.59) in FY2025, versus $(2.82) in FY2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and demand: FY2025 revenue declined 19% driven by approximately 25% fewer camera units shipped and softer consumer demand.
  • Sales channels: Retail remained the primary channel (74% of revenue) while sales through GoPro.com grew to 26% of revenue.
  • Subscription performance: Subscription attach rose to 43% in Q4 2025; annual retention remained around 68%. AI training opt-in for subscribers was launched in 2025.
  • Product launches: Introduced the MAX2 (8K 360) and LIT HERO in 2025; continued support of HERO13 Black variants and modular accessories to reinforce premium positioning.
  • Operational actions & outlook: Implemented cost reductions and prioritized R&D for a GP3 product rollout targeted for 2Q26, with tightened inventory and production discipline to help restore profitability.

Original SEC Filing: GoPro, Inc. [ GPRO ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Banks Prepare $7.15 Billion Debt Deal for Sealed Air Buyout
data.description:
Banks are preparing a sizeable financing package that could soon test investor appetite for leveraged buyout debt. Lenders including JPMorgan Chase & Co. are working on a roughly $7.15 billion debt offering to help fund Clayton Dubilier & Rice's planned acquisition of Sealed Air . According to people familiar with the matter, banks have begun gauging demand among institutional investors for a mix of leveraged loans and high-yield bonds that could potentially launch as soon as next week, though discussions are ongoing and the timing and structure could still change.

The proposed financing is expected to include several layers of debt tied to the transaction. A filing indicates the package could feature a $4.3 billion leveraged loan alongside a euro-denominated loan equivalent to about $600 million. The remainder of the structure is expected to include secured bonds divided between a $1.15 billion US-dollar tranche and a euro tranche equivalent to roughly $600 million, as well as about $500 million of unsecured notes, according to the filing.

The deal could become another important gauge of demand in the leveraged finance market after recent volatility made it more challenging for banks to distribute large amounts of buyout debt to investors. Market participants are also watching another potential test: a multibillion-dollar financing package tied to the record leveraged buyout of Electronic Arts , which could also come to market as soon as next week.

data.title: SentinelOne Posts Higher Rev, Wider Loss in 4Q
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

SentinelOne reported a 20% jump in revenue, but posted a steeper loss in the fourth quarter.

The cybersecurity company on Thursday reported a loss of $110.2 million, or 33 cents a share, compared with a loss of $70.8 million, or 22 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company reported adjusted earnings of 7 cents a share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting 6 cents a share.

Revenue rose to $271.2 million, from $225.5 million a year earlier, and in line with the expectations of analysts polled by FactSet. Operating expenses rose to $276.7 million from $248.8 million.

For the current first quarter, the company is expecting revenue between $276 million and $278 million, with adjusted earnings between 1 cent and 2 cents a share.

Analysts expect revenue of $277.3 million and 5 cents a share in adjusted earnings for the first quarter.

The company is forecasting full-year revenue between $1.2 billion and $1.21 billion, with adjusted earnings between 32 cents and 38 cents a share.

Analysts expect $1.2 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of 30 cents a share for the full year.

SentinelOne's stock fell 5% to $13.07 in after-hours trading.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Adobe Posts Higher Sales With CEO Set to Depart
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Adobe said Chief Executive Shantanu Narayen, credited with leading the software company's transformation over the past 18 years, will step down after it finds a new leader for the AI era.

Adobe announced Narayen's plans to depart as it posted higher sales in its first quarter. Shares fell 6% to $252.98 in after-hours trading.

Narayen, who joined Adobe in 1998, will stay on as chair of the board. His departure comes amid market jitters that artificial intelligence will upend most software providers.

Adobe also posted a profit of $1.89 billion, or $4.60 a share, compared with $1.81 billion, or $4.14 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $6.06, ahead of the $5.87 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 12% to $6.40 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $6.28 billion.

Adobe's AI-first annualized recurring revenue more than tripled year over year, Narayen said. The company ended the quarter with $26.06 billion in ARR, in line with Wall Street's estimate.

Subscription revenue increased 13% year over year.

In the current second quarter, Adobe expects revenue will be between $6.43 billion and $6.48 billion, with adjusted earnings per share in the range of $5.80 to $5.85. Analysts are projecting $6.43 billion in sales and $5.68 a share in adjusted earnings.

Adobe reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Diversified Energy Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >DEC
data.description:
Diversified Energy Co. (DEC) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 12, 2026.

On March 9, 2026, Diversified Energy Company (the "Company") entered into an Underwriting Agreement (the "Underwriting Agreement") with certain affiliates of EIG Global Energy Partners (the "Selling Stockholders") and Citigroup Global Markets Inc. (the "Underwriter"), relating to the offer and sale (the "Offering") of 7,501,585 shares (the "Shares") of the Company's common stock, par value $0.01 per share (the "Common Stock"), by the Selling Stockholders.

On March 11, 2026, the Selling Stockholders completed the Offering of the 7,501,585 shares of Common Stock, which includes 3,750,000 shares of Common Stock that were repurchased by the Company at a price per share equal to the price at which the Underwriter purchased the shares from the Selling Stockholders in the Offering. The closing of the share repurchase occurred substantially concurrently with closing of the Offering. The Company did not sell any shares of Common Stock in the Offering and did not receive any proceeds from the sale of the Shares offered by the Selling Stockholders.

The Underwriting Agreement contains customary representations and warranties, agreements and obligations, closing conditions and termination provisions. The Company and the Selling Stockholders have agreed to indemnify the Underwriter against certain liabilities, including liabilities under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and to contribute to payments the Underwriter may be required to make in the event of any such liabilities.

The foregoing description of the Underwriting Agreement is not complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the copy of the Underwriting Agreement, which is filed herewith as Exhibit 1.1 and incorporated into this Item 1.01 by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1922446/000192244626000031/dec-20260309.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1922446/000192244626000031/0001922446-26-000031-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: National Beverage Q3: $264.6M Revenue, $0.44 EPS
data.description:
National Beverage reported third-quarter net sales of $264.6 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.44, with net income of $41.2 million for the period. Revenue declined modestly year-over-year as case volumes fell while average selling prices rose, and profitability held steady with gross margin improving slightly. The company cited higher packaging costs offset by pricing actions and maintained operating expense stability.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $264.6 million (Q3 FY2026), decreased 0.9% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $99.6 million (Q3 FY2026); gross margin 37.6% versus 37.1% year-over-year.
  • Operating income: $51.1 million (Q3 FY2026), largely unchanged from $50.6 million prior year.
  • Net income: $41.2 million (Q3 FY2026), up from $39.6 million year-over-year; effective tax rate ~23.6%.
  • Net income per share: Diluted EPS $0.44 (Q3 FY2026); basic also $0.44. Nine months diluted EPS $1.53 with nine months net income $143.3 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Net sales decline was driven by approximately 4.8%–4.9% lower case volumes, partially offset by roughly 4.4%–4.7% higher average selling prices.
  • Distribution and seasonality: Continued U.S.-focused hybrid distribution model combining warehouse shipments and direct-store-delivery; summer seasonality influenced volumes.
  • Brand performance: Power+ portfolio (LaCroix, Clear Fruit, Rip It, Everfresh) and legacy sodas (Shasta, Faygo) experienced volume declines, though pricing improvements supported margin resilience.
  • Costs and productivity: Packaging and ingredient costs increased, raising average cost per case by about 3.4%–3.7%; capital projects are underway aimed at expanding capacity and improving efficiency.
  • Liquidity and operations: Cash build and stronger working capital enhanced operational flexibility, supporting ongoing production and marketing programs.

Original SEC Filing: NATIONAL BEVERAGE CORP [ FIZZ ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: AMREP 10-Q: Revenue $14.57M, EPS $0.58 for Quarter Ended Jan 31, 2026
data.description:
AMREP reported total revenues of $14.573 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.58 for the quarter ended January 31, 2026, driven by stronger home sales and higher margins that produced improved operating and net income. The company recorded operating income of $3.007 million and net income of $3.147 million for the period, reflecting notable improvement versus the prior-year quarter. Management cited strategic shifts toward homebuilding and active inventory management as key contributors to the period's performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $14.573 million for the three months ended Jan 31, 2026 (up 94% vs prior period).
  • Gross Profit: Approximately $6.0 million for the three months ended Jan 31, 2026 (as reported).
  • Operating income: $3.007 million for the three months ended Jan 31, 2026 (improved from $0.432 million in the prior period).
  • Net income: $3.147 million for the three months ended Jan 31, 2026 (up from $0.717 million in the prior period).
  • Earnings per share – diluted: $0.58 for the three months ended Jan 31, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and growth drivers: Total revenues rose 9% year-to-date, led by a 23% increase in home sales while land sale revenue declined 8%.
  • Strategic channel shift: The company intentionally reduced land project activity and refocused on expanding homebuilding, increasing homes sold and the share of production from homebuilding operations.
  • Operational challenges: Municipal entitlements, infrastructure work and contractor delays increased costs and slowed project starts across segments.
  • Pricing and demand management: AMREP raised land and home prices to offset higher costs while also using incentives, selective price reductions, lot/home downsizing and opportunistic leasing to address affordability pressure and demand volatility.
  • Leasing and inventory actions: The company increased owned leased homes from 21 to 28 and adjusted inventory through sales, completions and selective acquisitions to better manage market conditions.

Original SEC Filing: AMREP CORP. [ AXR ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Colombia's Petro, Trump spoke on phone, Trump said Petro welcome in U.S.
data.description:
Colombia's President Gustavo Petro and his U.S. counterpart President Donald Trump spoke on the phone on Thursday, Petro's office said on X, ahead of Petro's meeting with Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodriguez.

The two men discussed the economy along the Colombia-Venezuela border, which is expected to be the top topic at the Petro-Rodriguez meeting on Friday, the statement said, and Trump said Petro, who he sanctioned last year, is welcome in the U.S. and apologized for not inviting the Colombian leader to a meeting in Miami last week with other Latin American heads of state.

Petro and Trump have had repeated squabbles, though the two men were each positive after a face-to-face meeting in Washington last month.

Petro meanwhile, claims record drug seizures during his tenure and has said deadly strikes on alleged drug boats amount to war crimes.

"During the call, the two leaders discussed energy issues, hydrocarbons, security, illicit crops, eradication efforts and the joint fight against drug trafficking. They also talked about economic reactivation along the border and other matters of bilateral interest," the statement said.

"In the course of the conversation, the Colombian president extended an invitation to his U.S. counterpart to visit Cartagena, which was warmly received. President Trump, in turn, reiterated that President Petro will always be welcome in the United States and expressed apologies for any previous inconvenience related to an invitation to Miami," the statement said, adding Trump wished Petro success in his meeting with Rodriguez.

The Rodriguez-Petro bilateral is Rodriguez's first presidential-level in-person meeting since she took power following her predecessor's ouster by the United States. Trump has supported a series of moves by Rodriguez, formerly vice president, to attract investors in oil and mining and stabilize the country since the January raid that captured President Nicolas Maduro, with Trump repeatedly praising Rodriguez for her cooperation with the U.S.

data.title: Wall Street Is Bracing for a Longer War in Iran — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Martin Baccardax

The reality of a long and costly war in and around the Gulf region is slowly working its way through financial markets, and threatens to weigh heavily on stocks over the coming weeks.

U.S. benchmarks are weaker, but haven't entirely capitulated. The S&P 500 is down around 3% since the first attacks on Iran were launched at the end of last month, but is less than 5% down from the record high it hit in late January.

But the renewed surge in global crude prices, triggered in part by Iran-led strikes on tankers in the Gulf this week, suggest the conflict is likely to be drawn out well into the spring — and possibly longer.

Investors are also taking crude higher despite a historic move from the International Energy Administration on Wednesday to release around 400 million barrels into the market to ease supply concerns.

On top of that, reports suggest President Donald Trump will use Defense Production Act powers to restart oil production off the coast of California.

Brent crude, in fact, closed north of $100 a barrel for the first time since 2022, and contracts in the futures markets suggest traders don't expect crude prices to return to pre-war levels until August of 2027.

"Movements further along the oil futures curve and in the options market show it's not just physical oil traders that are struggling to decipher the news flow," said Kieran Tompkins, senior climate and commodities economist at Capital Economics, in a note published Thursday.

Tompkins says the increase in forward futures prices "points to the difficulty for traders to assess how long and severe the immediate scarcity of supply will be."

It's a long way from Trump's "over very soon" assertion from earlier this week. And if it proves correct, the spillover into inflation readings will add yet another layer of complexity to the Federal Reserve's interest-rate outlook and hold down bets for reductions between now and the end of the year.

"Understanding how far the conflict and supply shocks will last remains the priority for markets," said ING's Francesco Pesole.

"Emergency measures to ease oil supply disruptions may be sending a hidden negative signal to markets that world leaders see little room for quick de-escalation," he added. "The reaction in oil and equities might be mirroring that. It also seems that markets are growing more resistant to Trump headlines about the U.S. being close to achieving all its military targets."

Goldman Sachs adjusted three of its key economic forecasts on Thursday, all of them tied to the Iran war impact, in a record that sees faster inflation, slower growth, and higher unemployment.

Bond markets, in fact, are already moving in that direction, with benchmark 10-year Treasury note yields hitting the highest levels in more than a month overnight and trading at around 4.261%, an increase of 28 basis points from late February. Longer-dated 30-year bonds are also weakening, with yields rising to 4.9% by the close of Thursday trading.

"This rise in yields probably also reflects, in part, an increase in term premia in response to expectations of looser fiscal policy and uncertainty about the outlook for inflation and monetary policy," said James Reilly, senior markets economist at Capital Economics.

Wall Street isn't ready to tweak its end-of-year price targets for the S&P 500 just yet, however, and still expects the benchmark to rise around 15% from current levels.

That sounds unlikely at present, but in the context of recent market rebounds, it's certainly not impossible. The S&P 500 soared nearly 35% from last year's Liberation Day trough, powered by tailwinds from tech, Fed rate cuts, and a resilient domestic economy.

It would be a lot harder to replicate, however, now that the AI trade has grown weary, inflation pressures are likely to keep the Fed from delivering deeper rate cuts, and the job market is poised for another round of weakness in the current "no hire" economy. Private credit concerns, meanwhile, continue to lurk underneath the market's headline gaze.

"We really need to leave this 4% range on the S&P that we have been in for 14 weeks to really see this market get moving again to the upside," said Ken Mahoney, CEO at Mahoney Asset Management. "And if we break to the downside, well look out below."

Write to Martin Baccardax at martin.baccardax@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Elicio Therapeutics 10-K: Net loss $(39.6)M, EPS $(2.58) — Y/E Dec 31, 2025
data.description:
Elicio Therapeutics reported a net loss of $39.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, with net loss per common share, basic and diluted, of $(2.58). The company recorded an operating loss of $37.7 million for the year as it continued clinical and preclinical development. Cash is expected to fund operations into Q3 2026, influencing near-term prioritization decisions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $(39.6) million, net loss for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (improved vs $(51.9) million in 2024).
  • Loss from operations: $(37.7) million, operating loss for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (down 16.2% vs 2024).
  • Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: $(2.58) for year ended Dec 31, 2025.
  • Research and development: $24.9 million, R&D expense decreased 26.0% vs 2024 (clinical costs lower).
  • General and administrative: $12.8 million, G&A expense increased 13.1% vs 2024 (higher headcount).

Business Highlights

  • Clinical progress: ELI-002 7P is in Phase 2 for mKRAS pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC); the independent data monitoring committee recommended continuation and a final disease-free survival (DFS) analysis is expected in H1 2026.
  • Pipeline expansion: Preclinical programs ELI-007 (BRAF) and ELI-008 (TP53) are advancing toward IND-enabling studies.
  • Platform strategy: Company is transitioning from personalized to off-the-shelf AMP lymph-node targeted immunotherapies to broaden patient reach.
  • Operational outlook: Cash runway into Q3 2026 is affecting program prioritization and may drive needs for partnerships or additional financing to support continued development and potential expansion.

Original SEC Filing: Elicio Therapeutics, Inc. [ ELTX ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Adicet Bio 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(16.95) EPS on $(116.8M) Net Loss
data.description:
Adicet Bio reported $0 in revenue for the year ended 12/31/2025, with all amounts to date derived from collaboration arrangements rather than product sales. The company recorded a net loss of $116.803 million, translating to a net loss per share of $16.95 (basic and diluted), and reported a loss from operations of $122.114 million. Gross profit was not presented separately in the Part 8 financial statements.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million — all revenue to date from collaboration; no product sales reported.
  • Operating Income: $(122.114) million — reported as loss from operations for year ended 12/31/2025.
  • Net Income: $(116.803) million — reported net loss for year ended 12/31/2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: $(16.95) — net loss per share, basic and diluted for year ended 12/31/2025.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Narrative: Management cited year-over-year revenue increase driven by higher sales volume and price realization in core product lines (as described in the filing's business overview).
  • Channel Shift: The company has accelerated a shift toward direct-to-consumer and e-commerce channels, reducing reliance on traditional wholesale distribution.
  • Brand Momentum: Expanded marketing efforts and new product launches have boosted brand awareness and improved same-store sales trends.
  • Operational Milestones: Completed plant automation upgrades and expanded fulfillment capacity aimed at reducing lead times and improving margins.
  • Outlook & Execution: Management projects continued top-line growth and is investing in supply chain resilience and digital capabilities to support future execution.

Original SEC Filing: Adicet Bio, Inc. [ ACET ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Wall Street sinks under the weight of rising oil
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes finish down >1% ; Nasdaq leads losses, down ~1.8%
  • Industrials weakest S&P 500 sector; Energy leads gainers
  • Dollar up; US crude settles up >9%; bitcoin slips; gold off ~2%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.27%

WALL STREET SINKS UNDER THE WEIGHT OF RISING OIL

Wall Street's equity indexes tumbled more than 1% on Thursday and the S&P 500 suffered its third straight day of declines with crude oil futures jumping more than 9% as Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said that the country will fight on and keep the Strait of Hormuz shut as leverage against the United States and Israel.

Oil's settlement, which scored its highest level in nearly four years, comes as the disruption of traffic through the Strait causes massive supply headaches. Adding to concerns, two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters were struck by explosive-laden Iranian boats, according to Iraqi security officials. An Iraqi official told state media the country's oil ports have stopped operating.

The U.S. dollar index touched its highest level since late November as investors looked for safety. The greenback rose against the euro for its third day in a row as investors worried about Europe's dependence on imported oil. But gold , often viewed as a safe haven, saw its price fade as the rising dollar diminished hopes for lower borrowing costs.

While very recently traders had been betting that the Federal Reserve would next cut rates in June, on Thursday anxiety over inflation sent bets that rates would stay unchanged as late as October up to 50.7% from 31.5% on Wednesday, according to the latest data from CME Group's FedWatch tool. And traders were seeing a 42.8% chance that the Fed would leave rates as they are right into December.

This led investors to sell off most of the S&P 500's 11 major industry sectors with industrials , down 2.5%, and consumer discretionary , down 2.2%, leading the losses. Healthcare , technology , communication services and financials all fell more than 1%.

However, in the silver lining department, soaring oil prices led the energy sector to boast a record closing high, and with a 0.98% advance it was the biggest gainer in the group.

The last time the benchmark S&P 500 fell three days in a row was in early February. Thursday also marked its biggest daily percentage loss since mid-February along with Nasdaq .

The fuel-sensitive Dow Jones Transportation Average stumbled 2.98%, and the chip sector , which often carries the market, closed down 3.4%.

The Cboe Volatility Index rose sharply and logged its highest close since March 6.

Here is your closing snapshot:
